title: Closed cycle thermal energy storage system using thermochemical material
abstract: A closed-cycle thermal energy storage system includes a thermochemical reactor [400] containing a solid thermochemical material, a heat exchanger [402], a fan [406] for creating forced convection of an air/water mixture circulating around the closed cycle, an evaporator/condenser [408], and a pressure control system that includes a pressure sensor [422], a controller [424], and a pump [404]. The pressure control system actively controls the pressure of the air/water mixture to produce a desired temperature difference across the thermochemical reactor [400]. The adjustable pressure control system controls the pressure of the air/water mixture to a first pressure (e.g., between 10 mbar and 1 bar) during a hydration phase and a second pressure (e.g., between 1 bar and 10 bar) during a dehydration phase.
claims:
Claims 1. A closed-cycle thermal energy storage system comprising: a thermochemical reactor containing a solid thermochemical material, a heat exchanger, an evaporator/condenser, conduits connecting the reactor, heat exchanger, and evaporator to allow convection of an air/water mixture circulating around the close cycle, and a pressure control system for actively controlling the pressure of the air/water mixture to produce a desired temperature difference across the thermochemical reactor. 2. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 wherein the pressure control system controls the pressure of the air/water mixture to a first pressure during a hydration phase and a second pressure during a dehydration phase. 3. The thermal energy storage system of claim 2 wherein the first pressure and the second pressure are overpressures between 1 bar and below 10 bar. 4. The thermal energy storage system of claim 2 wherein the first pressure is an underpressure in the range 10 mbar to 1 bar and the second pressure is an overpressure in the range 1 bar to 10 bar. 5. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 wherein the pressure control system comprises a pressure sensor, a controller, and a pump, wherein the controller is configured to adjust the pump to create a desired system pressure based in part on measurements from the pressure sensor. 6. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 wherein the thermochemical material is a material selected from salts (hydrates/ammoniates/methanol complexes), molecular sieves (zeolites), metal organic frameworks (MOFs), silicagel, or activated alumina. 7. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 further comprising a gas property sensor that senses a physical characteristic of the air/water mixture, wherein the adjustable 

 pressure control system controls the pressure of the air/water mixture based in part on measurements from the gas property sensor. 8. The thermal energy storage system of claim 7 wherein the gas property sensor is a pressure sensor. 9. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 further comprising two relative humidity sensors positioned on opposite sides of the thermochemical reactor, wherein the pressure control system controls the pressure of the air/water mixture based in part on measurements from the two relative humidity sensors. 10. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 wherein the thermochemical reactor and the heat exchanger are distinct components. 11. The thermal energy storage system of claim 1 further comprising a fan connected to the conduits to force convection of the air/water mixture circulating around the close cycle. 12. A method of operating a closed cycle thermal energy storage system comprising a thermochemical reactor containing a solid thermochemical material, the method comprising: a) determining from a gas property sensor a pressure of an air/water mixture circulating in the system, b) actively controlling the pressure of the air/water mixture to produce a desired temperature difference across the thermochemical reactor. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the pressure of the air/water mixture is controlled to be a first pressure during a hydration phase and a second pressure during a dehydration phase. 14. The method of claim 13 wherein the first pressure is an underpressure between and 1 bar and the second pressure is an overpressure between 1 bar and 10 bar. 115. The method of claim 12 wherein actively controlling the pressure comprises measuring a physical property of the air/water mixture and controlling a pump to create a desired system pressure based in part on the measured physical property of the air/water mixture. 16. The method of claim 15 wherein measuring a physical property of the air/water mixture comprises measuring a pressure of the air/water mixture.
